description: delegation whose scopes list contains a string that does not parse per SPEC §7.1 grammar — verifier MUST reject with E_BAD_SCOPE_GRAMMAR per §8.1 step 4

expected.rejection_reason: The scope string `lock:seal(recipient bc1qalice…)` is missing the `=` (or `<=`, `<`, etc.) operator between the constraint key and value. Per §7.1, `<constraint>` ::= `<key> <op> <value>`, where `<op>` is one of `=`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `*`. A space alone is not a valid operator. Conformant verifiers MUST refuse to compute an `id` for this delegation and MUST NOT proceed past §8.1 step 4.

additional_malformed_examples_for_implem.why: empty parentheses with no constraints is allowed only as `lock:seal` (no parens); empty parens are not in the grammar
empty value for `recipient` — §7.1 requires <value> to be a non-empty <bare-token> | <quoted-string> | <integer> | <url>
case violation — §7.1 / §7.2 require lowercase product and verb
ordered op `<=` with non-integer value — §7.4 numeric comparison requires decimal integers

harness_assertion: Given any of the malformed scopes above as input, a conformant implementation MUST refuse to construct the delegation envelope. The implementation MAY surface the rejection at envelope-construction time (preferred) or at verify time, but the error code in either case is E_BAD_SCOPE_GRAMMAR.
